message:
fix(redis): prevent memory exhaustion from oversized arrays and credential leaks

Three Redis client hardening fixes:

1. Array length guard: Cap RESP array declarations at 1M elements.
   A malicious or corrupted server could send *999999999\r\n, causing
   Vec::with_capacity to attempt a multi-gigabyte allocation before
   any elements are even parsed.

2. Password redaction in Debug: Replace the derived Debug impl on
   RedisConfig with a manual implementation that prints passwords as
   "[REDACTED]", preventing accidental credential exposure in logs
   and error messages.

3. URL password parsing: Change split_once('@') to rsplit_once('@')
   so that passwords containing literal '@' characters are parsed
   correctly (the authority delimiter is the last '@' in the URL).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
